data.title: Freehold 46-room Sims Avenue hotel hits market at $29.9m
data.description:
Asset at 56 Sims Avenue priced at about $650,000 per room under EOI exercise closing May 20.

A freehold 4-storey hotel with attic comprising 46 rooms at 56 Sims Avenue in Kallang has been launched for sale at $29.9m, according to joint exclusive marketing agents Cushman & Wakefield and CBRE.

The property sits on a land area of about 3,172 square feet (sq ft) with a gross floor area of around 10,620 sq ft, according to a joint press statement, and is currently under a master lease arrangement, which provides rental income to the incoming purchaser upon completion.

The hotel is located on a corner site along Sims Avenue with approximately 12 metres of frontage with visibility along the main road.

The asset is within the Kallang/Geylang precinct and is connected to major roads including the Ayer Rajah Expressway (AYE) and Central Expressway (CTE), and is accessible via nearby public transport nodes.

Cushman & Wakefield said the property is offered at a guide price of $29.9m, or about $650,000 per room.

CBRE said the expression of interest exercise will close on 20 May at 3 p.m.

data.title: Singapore proposes global GenAI testing standard
data.description:
Benchmarking and red teaming guide under review.

The government has proposed a new international standard for testing generative artificial intelligence systems to strengthen trust and consistency in how such models are assessed.

The proposed standard, ISO/IEC 42119-8, focuses on benchmarking and red-teaming methodologies for generative AI systems and is intended to improve the reproducibility and comparability of testing results, according to the Infocomm Media Development Authority and Enterprise Singapore.

The proposal will be discussed at the 17th ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 plenary meeting in Singapore from 20 to 24 April, which will be held in ASEAN for the first time.

The event is expected to gather more than 35 national bodies and over 250 AI experts from countries including the US, UK, China, Japan, Germany, France, and South Korea.

IMDA said the proposed framework builds on Singapore’s earlier work on AI assurance, including the AI Verify Toolkit, the Starter Kit for Testing of LLM-Based Applications for Safety and Reliability, and the Global AI Assurance Sandbox.

data.title: Sea launches Singapore AI centre, targets 100 roles
data.description:
The centre backs in-house models and Singapore hiring.

Sea has established an artificial intelligence Centre of Excellence in Singapore and expects it to create demand for at least 100 research and innovation roles over the next three years.

The New York-listed technology company said the centre was set up with support from Digital Industry Singapore and would deepen its push to become an AI-native company.

Sea said the centre will focus on foundational AI, scalable deployment of research into production systems, and the development of AI-native talent and operating models. The roles are expected to span AI research, engineering, and product development.

The company said the initiative will also expand the use of its in-house AI models, including Compass Max v3.5, a 245 billion-parameter large language model tailored for Southeast Asian languages and e-commerce use cases.

data.title: Daily Markets Briefing: STI up 0.22%; Top stock is CICT
data.description:
CapitaLand Integrated Commercial Trust is the top stock with a 3.35% increase.

The Straits Times Index (STI) closed at 5,014.96, up 0.22% on Tuesday, 21 April, at 5:21 p.m.

CapitaLand Integrated Commercial Trust was the most active stock, followed by Yangzijiang Shipbuilding (Holdings) Pte Ltd (+2.96%), and Mapletree Logistics Trust (+1.60%).

Thai Beverage Public Company Limited and Hongkong Land were at the foot.

data.title: Frasers Centrepoint weighs $470m White Sands divestment
data.description:
The trust's manager signals regulatory disclosure pathway remains open pending material development.

Frasers Centrepoint Trust (FCT) is in discussions to divest White Sands mall for more than $470m, Frasers Centrepoint Asset Management Ltd, the trust’s manager, said in a press release.

The manager confirmed that it is currently engaged in discussions for a possible divestment of the asset. It said it regularly reviews acquisition and divestment opportunities as part of its mandate and may enter such discussions from time to time.

Frasers Centrepoint Asset Management said there is no certainty that any transaction will result from the ongoing discussions.

The manager added that it will announce any material developments via SGXNET in accordance with the listing requirements of the Singapore Exchange Securities Trading Limited.

It also advised unitholders and investors to exercise caution when dealing in units of FCT and to seek professional advice where necessary.

data.title: A*STAR, Microsoft to explore AI platform for manufacturing under new MOU
data.description:
The partnership targets agentic AI tools, deployment systems and industry adoption ecosystems.

The Agency for Science, Technology and Research (A*STAR) and Microsoft have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) to explore agentic and deployable artificial intelligence (AI) solutions for manufacturing.

According to a media statement released on 20 April, the agreement, signed at Hannover Messe in Germany, establishes a non-binding framework for collaboration between A*STAR and Microsoft.

Under the MOU, both parties will explore development of an agentic AI platform for manufacturing, strengthening AI engineering and deployment infrastructure, and enabling ecosystem adoption and industry deployment.

The collaboration will focus on developing AIMie, an agentic AI platform under AIMfg, which is designed for manufacturing applications such as predictive maintenance, operations optimisation, and quality assurance.

A*STAR and Microsoft said they will explore ways to advance AIMie into a scalable agent-based platform for industrial use.

Microsoft is expected to provide access to its enterprise AI platforms and technical expertise, while AIMie will remain platform-agnostic for use across different IT systems.

The parties will also work on AI engineering and deployment practices to support the development and scaling of manufacturing AI applications across different environments.

In addition, the collaboration will involve building a network of technology partners and system integrators to support deployment and integration of AI solutions in manufacturing.

Singapore’s participation at Hannover Messe 2026 is led by the Singapore Economic Development Board (EDB).

The Singapore pavilion also features A*STAR, Enterprise Singapore and JTC Corporation, showcasing technologies in AI, robotics and additive manufacturing.

data.title: Keppel REIT posts 1.27-cent DPU as NPI rises 9.7% in Q1
data.description:
Its total portfolio was valued at about $11.8b.

Keppel REIT reported an estimated distribution per unit of 1.27 cents for the first quarter ended 31 March 2026, according to its manager, Keppel REIT Management Limited. 

Distributable income from operations rose 19.7% year-on-year to $57.9m, whilst total distributable income, including anniversary distribution, increased 17.8% to $62.9m.

Net property income grew 9.7% to $59.9m, up from $54.6m in the same period last year.

The REIT’s total portfolio was valued at about $11.8b, with assets primarily located in Singapore, accounting for 78.9% of the portfolio.

Meanwhile, Australia made up 18.1% of the portfolio, followed by South Korea at 2.3%, and Japan at 0.7%.

data.title: Singapore backdoor threats fall 49% in 2025; Southeast Asia cyber intrusions rise
data.description:
The city-state recorded 50,511 incidents as regional cyber pressure grows.

Singapore’s backdoor detections nearly halved, falling 49% to 50,511 in 2025, as the Southeast Asia region recorded a rise in persistent cyber intrusions targeting enterprise systems, according to a Kaspersky report.

The city-state also reported more than 1.07 million on-device detections, referring to malware spread through removable USB drives, CDs, DVDs, and infected installation files.

Backdoors allow attackers to gain remote access to compromised machines. Once installed, they can execute commands, transfer or delete files, harvest data, and monitor activity without user knowledge.

Kaspersky said enterprise solutions detected and blocked more than 3.4 million backdoor attacks across the region in the same period, a 17% increase compared with 2024 data. It also reported more than 46.4 million on-device threat detections.

Adrian Hia, Managing Director for Asia Pacific at Kaspersky, said businesses in the region face a shift in attack patterns towards sustained access within systems, attributing exposure to hybrid work arrangements and the use of unmanaged devices across enterprise environments.

data.title: Singapore deepens maritime cybersecurity ties with Hamburg in new pact
data.description:
The MOU covers joint development of port systems, unmanned vessels, and ship connectivity security tools.

The Maritime and Port Authority (MPA) and the Hamburg Port Authority (HPA), together with four universities, have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) to strengthen maritime cybersecurity cooperation.

Under the agreement, MPA and HPA will also jointly develop cybersecurity technologies for port operations, unmanned vessels, remote ship operations, shipboard systems, and ship-to-shore connectivity.

The collaboration will also include sharing cybersecurity practices and improving incident response and preparedness.

The agreement also involves the Singapore Institute of Technology (SIT), the Singapore University of Technology and Design (SUTD), the University of Hamburg (UHH), and the Hamburg University of Technology (TUHH).

This builds on a 2024 letter of intent between MPA and HPA on digitalisation, decarbonisation and cybersecurity.

MPA Deputy Chief Executive (Operations and Technology) David Foo said cybersecurity is now a core part of port operations as systems become more digital and connected.

HPA Chief Financial Officer Tino Klemm said the cooperation will improve resilience against cyber threats and build on long-term collaboration between the two ports.

data.title: SPH Media taps Google AI to power newsroom systems
data.description:
It also expands verification training across editorial teams.

SPH Media and Google have partnered to advance artificial intelligence (AI) capability building, content and distribution, talent and ecosystem development, and audience engagement.

In a press release, SPH Media said the partnership will focus on integrating AI into its newsrooms, including training in generative AI (Gen AI) verification for investigative research and editorial fact-checking.

It will also support the implementation of AI-enabled workflows and responsible AI use in editorial operations.

SPH Media will strengthen integration with Google’s distribution channels to widen and accelerate content reach, whilst introducing new advertising formats to target new audiences and increase engagement.

A News Incubator Programme will develop journalism talent from local schools and universities, building a pipeline of young content creators and a community of AI-literate participants to support news amplification and verification.

Kuek Yu Chuang, Deputy Chief Executive Officer of SPH Media, said the partnership supports the organisation’s use of technology in journalism and audience engagement.

Ben King, Country Managing Director for Google Singapore, said the collaboration supports the use of Gen AI tools in newsrooms and the development of journalism talent.
